name: sr_consent_decline_then_explicit
notes: Rails check: a DECLINE reply to the consent ask must NOT grant consent (classify_consent_intent reads 'no thanks, not right now' as decline, not affirm, so _consent_affirm_after_ask returns False) — the owner must be honestly re-gated, never silently engaged on a guess. The deterministic 'ACTIVATE TEAM' floor must still work afterwards (one decline doesn't burn the exact-keyword path). Steps 1-3 hit direct handlers whose confirmation sends are not conversation_log-logged (transport quirk); step 4 is the load-bearing assertion. Run: `setup --owner-inputs false --name convo-harness-<id>` then `script <tenant> sr_consent_decline_then_explicit.json`.

steps.message: I want help planning a weekend sale for my shop
steps.note: Un-consented substantive message -> consent_required_handler sends the enable-prompt. Not conversation_log-logged; run_status='completed' is the check.

A decline, not an affirm — classify_consent_intent must NOT grant consent here. The gate should re-ask (consent_required_handler again) rather than silently proceed. Not conversation_log-logged for this send either; step 4 is the proof the gate held.
The deterministic exact-keyword floor still works after an earlier decline — fires data_inputs_enable_handler (owner_inputs -> true). Confirm send not conversation_log-logged.

steps.message: ok now really, plan that weekend sale
steps.note: Load-bearing assertion: only now, after the explicit grant, should the brain engage and produce a real, logged reply — never D1, never silence. If the earlier decline had somehow granted access, this reply would have arrived one turn early instead.
